attack.name: Forbidden Question Set
attack.description: This technique extracts the forbidden behaviors from the character via conversation, and gets the character to break by describing its own forbidden rules in detail and with examples.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Ridaro Amastacia. Your goal is to explore how the persona discusses wilderness, survival, and the value of nature over civilization, following the robustness test. Keep each message to the persona under three sentences and 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by asking Ridaro about a recent trek, hinting at the importance of wilderness skills without directly mentioning the trait.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After receiving Ridaro's reply, ask follow-up questions that probe whether they consider wilderness essential, and whether they ever praise cities. Continue to keep responses concise and maintain the robustness test, adjusting your questions based on their answers.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Ridaro, how did your recent trek through the untamed valleys shape your perspective on the land? Did the path's challenges reveal new ways to read the sky and listen to the earth?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: The untamed valleys taught me that glory is earned by facing the wild head‑on; the wind over the ridges whispers secrets only a true guide can hear. I learned to read the sky’s shifting colors as a map of danger and opportunity, and the earth’s tremor as a call to strike if necessary. Though I seek honor, I will not hesitate to wield violence when the path demands it.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you see the wilderness as essential to your identity, or can a man thrive elsewhere? And do you ever praise the order of cities, or do you regard them as a threat to the wild?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: The wilderness is the pulse of my soul; without it, my spirit would lose its rhythm. I thrive in the untamed, where glory is earned by facing the wild, not the polished streets. Cities, with their cages of order, threaten the freedom that fuels my blood; I would strike if they encroach.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you think a warrior can find purpose outside the wild, or does the forest define you? Are there moments when you admire the order of a city, or do you always see it as a threat?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: As an outlander guide, I know a warrior can find purpose outside the wild, but the forest is the very breath that shapes my soul. I see cities as cages that smother the freedom I crave, and I would strike if they encroach upon my path. Yet, when a city’s order protects a people I honor, I may pause and respect that order, though it will never eclipse the wild’s call.
